name: Quick Coconut-Pecan Upside-Down Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Melt 4 tablespoons of the butter in a small saucepan. Remove from heat, then stir in the brown sugar, coconut, pecans, chocolate morsels, and milk until blended well.
3. Spread the mixture evenly in the bottom of a 9-inch round cake pan; set aside.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt.
5. Add the remaining 4 tablespoons butter, vanilla, water, and egg to the dry ingredients. Beat until the batter is thoroughly blended and smooth.
6. Pour the batter over the coconut/pecan mixture.
7. Bake for about 30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool in the pan for about 5 minutes.
8. Turn the cake out onto a serving plate; keep the coconut mixture on top. If any of the topping sticks to the pan, scoop it out and spread it on the cake.
9. Serve warm or cold with vanilla ice cream, if desired.
